file.content:
# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
# Internal: parse / render taxonomy headers
# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

# Parse a single FASTA header string (with or without leading ">") into a
# named list: id (character) + ranks (named character vector, e.g. c(k="Fungi")).
# Supported formats: "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2".
.parse_tax_header <- function(header, format) {
  h <- sub("^>", "", header)

  if (format == "sintax") {
    m <- regexpr(";tax=", h, fixed = TRUE)
    if (m < 0) {
      return(list(id = h, ranks = character(0)))
    }
    id <- substr(h, 1, m - 1L)
    rank_str <- substr(h, m + 5L, nchar(h))
    rank_pairs <- strsplit(rank_str, ",", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    keys <- vapply(
      rank_pairs,
      \(rp) strsplit(rp, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]][1],
      character(1)
    )
    vals <- vapply(
      rank_pairs,
      \(rp) {
        kv <- strsplit(rp, ":", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
        if (length(kv) > 1L) kv[2L] else NA_character_
      },
      character(1)
    )
    ranks <- stats::setNames(vals, keys)
    list(id = id, ranks = ranks)
  } else if (format %in% c("unite", "greengenes2")) {
    if (format == "unite") {
      parts <- strsplit(h, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
      id <- parts[1L]
      rank_parts <- parts[-1L]
    } else {
      # greengenes2: space separates ID from taxonomy string
      space_pos <- regexpr("\\s", h)
      if (space_pos < 0L) {
        return(list(id = h, ranks = character(0)))
      }
      id <- substr(h, 1L, space_pos - 1L)
      tax_str <- trimws(substr(h, space_pos + 1L, nchar(h)))
      rank_parts <- strsplit(tax_str, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    }
    keys <- vapply(
      rank_parts,
      \(rp) strsplit(rp, "__", fixed = TRUE)[[1]][1L],
      character(1)
    )
    vals <- vapply(
      rank_parts,
      \(rp) {
        kv <- strsplit(rp, "__", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
        if (length(kv) > 1L) paste(kv[-1L], collapse = "__") else NA_character_
      },
      character(1)
    )
    ranks <- stats::setNames(vals, keys)
    list(id = id, ranks = ranks)
  } else {
    stop(
      "Unsupported input format: '",
      format,
      "'. ",
      "Use one of: 'sintax', 'unite', 'greengenes2'."
    )
  }
}


# Render a parsed header (list with id + ranks) to the target format string
# (without leading ">").
.render_tax_header <- function(parsed, format) {
  id <- parsed$id
  ranks <- parsed$ranks

  if (format == "sintax") {
    if (length(ranks) == 0L) {
      return(id)
    }
    rank_str <- paste(paste0(names(ranks), ":", ranks), collapse = ",")
    paste0(id, ";tax=", rank_str)
  } else if (format == "unite") {
    if (length(ranks) == 0L) {
      return(id)
    }
    rank_str <- paste(paste0(names(ranks), "__", ranks), collapse = ";")
    paste0(id, ";", rank_str)
  } else if (format == "greengenes2") {
    if (length(ranks) == 0L) {
      return(id)
    }
    rank_str <- paste(paste0(names(ranks), "__", ranks), collapse = ";")
    paste0(id, " ", rank_str)
  } else if (format == "dada2") {
    # Unprefixed semicolon-separated taxonomy, trailing semicolon, no ID
    if (length(ranks) == 0L) {
      return(paste0(id, ";"))
    }
    paste0(paste(ranks, collapse = ";"), ";")
  } else if (format == "dada2_species") {
    # "ID Genus Species" for dada2::addSpecies()
    g <- ranks[names(ranks) == "g"]
    s <- ranks[names(ranks) == "s"]
    if (length(g) == 0L || is.na(g)) {
      g <- ""
    }
    if (length(s) == 0L || is.na(s)) {
      s <- ""
    }
    paste(id, g, s)
  } else {
    stop(
      "Unsupported output format: '",
      format,
      "'. ",
      "Use one of: 'sintax', 'unite', 'greengenes2', 'dada2', 'dada2_species'."
    )
  }
}


# Detect format from a plain text string (not a file path).
.detect_tax_format_str <- function(text) {
  if (grepl("tax=", text, fixed = TRUE)) {
    return("sintax")
  }
  if (grepl("d__", text, fixed = TRUE)) {
    return("greengenes2")
  }
  if (grepl("k__", text, fixed = TRUE)) {
    return("unite")
  }
  "unknown"
}


# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
# format_fasta_db(): unified conversion function
# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

#' Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format
#'
#' @description
#' Detects (or uses) the input taxonomy format and rewrites sequence headers
#' to the requested output format. This is the primary conversion function;
#' [format2sintax()], [format2dada2()], and [format2dada2_species()] are
#' convenience wrappers around it.
#'
#' Supported **input** formats (prefix-based, with detectable rank labels):
#' `"sintax"`, `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`.
#'
#' Supported **output** formats:
#' - `"sintax"` — VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX (`>ID;tax=k:Kingdom,p:Phylum,...`)
#' - `"unite"` — UNITE default (`>ID;k__Kingdom;p__Phylum;...`)
#' - `"greengenes2"` — Greengenes2 (`>ID d__Domain;p__Phylum;...`)
#' - `"dada2"` — Unprefixed semicolon-delimited (`>Kingdom;Phylum;...;`)
#' - `"dada2_species"` — For `dada2::addSpecies()` (`>ID Genus Species`)
#'
#' Positional formats (`"pr2"`, `"dada2"`) can be detected by
#' [detect_tax_format()] but cannot be used as input for conversion because
#' they lack rank labels.
#'
#' @param fasta_db (Character) Path to a FASTA file (plain or gzipped).
#'   Mutually exclusive with `taxnames`.
#' @param taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without
#'   leading `>`). Mutually exclusive with `fasta_db`.
#' @param output_format (Character) Target format. One of `"sintax"`,
#'   `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`, `"dada2"`, `"dada2_species"`.
#' @param input_format (Character, default `"auto"`) Input format. One of
#'   `"auto"` (auto-detect via [detect_tax_format()]), `"sintax"`,
#'   `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`.
#' @param output_path (Character) If provided and `fasta_db` is used, write
#'   the reformatted FASTA to this path and return the `DNAStringSet`
#'   invisibly.
#'
#' @returns If `taxnames` is used, a character vector of reformatted headers.
#'   If `fasta_db` is used, a `DNAStringSet` with reformatted names
#'   (invisibly when `output_path` is given).
#' @export
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#' @seealso [detect_tax_format()], [format2sintax()], [format2dada2()],
#'   [format2dada2_species()]
#' @examples
#' # UNITE → SINTAX
#' format_fasta_db(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota;c__Sordariomycetes",
#'   output_format = "sintax"
#' )
#'
#' # SINTAX → UNITE
#' format_fasta_db(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;tax=k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota,c:Sordariomycetes",
#'   output_format = "unite"
#' )
#'
#' # Greengenes2 → dada2
#' format_fasta_db(
#'   taxnames = "abc123 d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;g__Escherichia",
#'   output_format = "dada2"
#' )
format_fasta_db <- function(
  fasta_db = NULL,
  taxnames = NULL,
  output_format = c("sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species"),
  input_format = "auto",
  output_path = NULL
) {
  output_format <- match.arg(output_format)

  if (is.null(taxnames) && is.null(fasta_db)) {
    stop("You must specify taxnames or fasta_db parameter.")
  }
  if (!is.null(taxnames) && !is.null(fasta_db)) {
    stop("You must specify either taxnames or fasta_db, not both.")
  }

  if (!is.null(taxnames)) {
    if (input_format == "auto") {
      input_format <- .detect_tax_format_str(paste(taxnames, collapse = " "))
      if (input_format == "unknown") {
        stop(
          "Cannot auto-detect input format from taxnames. ",
          "Please specify `input_format`."
        )
      }
    }
    parsed <- lapply(taxnames, .parse_tax_header, format = input_format)
    vapply(parsed, \(p) .render_tax_header(p, output_format), character(1))
  } else {
    if (input_format == "auto") {
      input_format <- detect_tax_format(fasta_db)
      if (input_format == "unknown") {
        stop(
          "Cannot auto-detect input format. ",
          "Please specify `input_format`."
        )
      }
    }
    dna <- Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(fasta_db)
    parsed <- lapply(names(dna), .parse_tax_header, format = input_format)
    new_names <- vapply(
      parsed,
      \(p) .render_tax_header(p, output_format),
      character(1)
    )
    names(dna) <- new_names
    if (!is.null(output_path)) {
      Biostrings::writeXStringSet(dna, filepath = output_path)
      invisible(dna)
    } else {
      dna
    }
  }
}


# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
# format2sintax(), format2dada2(), format2dada2_species()
# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————

#' Format taxonomy headers to SINTAX format
#'
#' @description
#' Converts taxonomy headers to the VSEARCH SINTAX format
#' (`>ID;tax=k:Kingdom,p:Phylum,...`). Wrapper around [format_fasta_db()].
#'
#' @param fasta_db (Character) Path to a FASTA file. Mutually exclusive
#'   with `taxnames`.
#' @param taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without
#'   leading `>`). Mutually exclusive with `fasta_db`.
#' @param input_format (Character, default `"auto"`) Input taxonomy format.
#'   One of `"auto"`, `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`, `"sintax"`.
#' @param output_path (Character) If provided and `fasta_db` is used, write
#'   the reformatted FASTA to this path.
#'
#' @returns If `taxnames` is used, a character vector of reformatted names.
#'   If `fasta_db` is used, a `DNAStringSet` with reformatted names.
#' @export
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#' @seealso [format_fasta_db()], [format2dada2()], [format2dada2_species()]
#' @examples
#' # UNITE format → SINTAX
#' format2sintax(taxnames = "AB123;k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota;c__Sordariomycetes")
#'
#' # Greengenes2 format → SINTAX
#' format2sintax(
#'   taxnames = "abc123 d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota",
#'   input_format = "greengenes2"
#' )
format2sintax <- function(
  fasta_db = NULL,
  taxnames = NULL,
  input_format = "auto",
  output_path = NULL
) {
  format_fasta_db(
    fasta_db = fasta_db,
    taxnames = taxnames,
    output_format = "sintax",
    input_format = input_format,
    output_path = output_path
  )
}


#' Format taxonomy headers for dada2::assignTaxonomy
#'
#' @description
#' Converts taxonomy headers to the format expected by
#' `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`: unprefixed semicolon-delimited taxonomy
#' (`>Kingdom;Phylum;Class;Order;Family;Genus;`). Wrapper around
#' [format_fasta_db()].
#'
#' @param fasta_db (Character) Path to a FASTA file. Mutually exclusive
#'   with `taxnames`.
#' @param taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without
#'   leading `>`). Mutually exclusive with `fasta_db`.
#' @param input_format (Character, default `"auto"`) Input taxonomy format.
#'   One of `"auto"`, `"sintax"`, `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`.
#' @param output_path (Character) If provided and `fasta_db` is used, write
#'   the reformatted FASTA to this path. The `DNAStringSet` is returned
#'   invisibly.
#' @param pattern_to_remove (Character) Optional regex pattern to remove
#'   from the reformatted names (applied after conversion).
#'
#' @returns If `taxnames` is used, a character vector. If `fasta_db` is
#'   used, a `DNAStringSet` with reformatted names. When `output_path` is
#'   provided, returned invisibly.
#' @export
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#' @seealso [format_fasta_db()], [format2sintax()], [format2dada2_species()]
#' @examples
#' # SINTAX format → dada2
#' format2dada2(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;tax=k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota,c:Sordariomycetes"
#' )
#'
#' # UNITE format → dada2
#' format2dada2(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota;c__Sordariomycetes",
#'   input_format = "unite"
#' )
format2dada2 <- function(
  fasta_db = NULL,
  taxnames = NULL,
  input_format = "auto",
  output_path = NULL,
  pattern_to_remove = NULL
) {
  result <- format_fasta_db(
    fasta_db = fasta_db,
    taxnames = taxnames,
    output_format = "dada2",
    input_format = input_format,
    output_path = output_path
  )
  if (!is.null(pattern_to_remove)) {
    if (is.character(result)) {
      result <- stringr::str_remove(result, pattern_to_remove)
    } else {
      names(result) <- stringr::str_remove(names(result), pattern_to_remove)
    }
  }
  result
}


#' Format taxonomy headers for dada2::addSpecies
#'
#' @description
#' Converts taxonomy headers to the format expected by
#' `dada2::addSpecies()`: `ID Genus Species`. Wrapper around
#' [format_fasta_db()].
#'
#' @param fasta_db (Character) Path to a FASTA file. Mutually exclusive
#'   with `taxnames`.
#' @param taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without
#'   leading `>`). Mutually exclusive with `fasta_db`.
#' @param input_format (Character, default `"auto"`) Input taxonomy format.
#'   One of `"auto"`, `"sintax"`, `"unite"`, `"greengenes2"`.
#' @param output_path (Character) If provided and `fasta_db` is used, write
#'   the reformatted FASTA to this path.
#'
#' @returns If `taxnames` is used, a character vector. If `fasta_db` is
#'   used, a `DNAStringSet` with reformatted names.
#' @export
#' @author Adrien Taudière
#' @seealso [format_fasta_db()], [format2sintax()], [format2dada2()]
#' @examples
#' # UNITE format → dada2_species
#' format2dada2_species(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;k__Fungi;g__Aspergillus;s__fumigatus"
#' )
#'
#' # SINTAX format → dada2_species
#' format2dada2_species(
#'   taxnames = "AB123;tax=k:Fungi,g:Aspergillus,s:fumigatus",
#'   input_format = "sintax"
#' )
format2dada2_species <- function(
  fasta_db = NULL,
  taxnames = NULL,
  input_format = "auto",
  output_path = NULL
) {
  format_fasta_db(
    fasta_db = fasta_db,
    taxnames = taxnames,
    output_format = "dada2_species",
    input_format = input_format,
    output_path = output_path
  )
}

2	# Internal download helper
3	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
4	
5	#' Download a file with progress and validation
6	#'
7	#' @param url The URL to download from.
8	#' @param dest_path The local file path to save to.
9	#' @param verbose Print progress messages.
10	#'
11	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
12	#' @keywords internal
13	download_file <- function(url, dest_path, verbose = TRUE) {
14	  if (verbose) {
15	    message("Downloading from:\n  ", url)
16	    message("Saving to:\n  ", dest_path)
17	  }
18	
19	  tryCatch(
20	    {
21	      utils::download.file(
22	        url,
23	        destfile = dest_path,
24	        mode = "wb",
25	        quiet = !verbose
26	      )
27	    },
28	    error = function(e) {
29	      if (file.exists(dest_path)) {
30	        unlink(dest_path)
31	      }
32	      stop(
33	        "Download failed. Check your internet connection and that the URL ",
34	        "is still valid:\n  ",
35	        url,
36	        "\nOriginal error: ",
37	        conditionMessage(e),
38	        call. = FALSE
39	      )
40	    }
41	  )
42	
43	  if (!file.exists(dest_path) || file.size(dest_path) == 0) {
44	    if (file.exists(dest_path)) {
45	      unlink(dest_path)
46	    }
47	    stop(
48	      "Download produced an empty file. The URL may be invalid:\n  ",
49	      url,
50	      call. = FALSE
51	    )
52	  }
53	
54	  if (verbose) {
55	    size_mb <- round(file.size(dest_path) / 1024^2, 1)
56	    message("Download complete (", size_mb, " MB)")
57	  }
58	
59	  invisible(dest_path)
60	}

63	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
64	# UNITE
65	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
66	
67	#' Download a UNITE reference database
68	#'
69	#' @description
70	#' Downloads the UNITE fungal ITS database for taxonomic assignment from
71	#' the UNITE DOI repository (PlutoF/Zenodo). Provides both "dynamic"
72	#' (including singletons) and "static" versions, for fungi or all eukaryotes.
73	#'
74	#' UNITE general FASTA releases use the `k__`/`p__` taxonomy format and
75	#' are compatible with dada2, VSEARCH, and other classifiers after
76	#' reformatting with [format2dada2()] or [format2sintax()].
77	#'
78	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
79	#'   downloaded file.
80	#' @param type (Character, default `"dynamic"`) One of `"dynamic"` or
81	#'   `"static"`. Dynamic files include singletons. Only used when
82	#'   `taxonomic_format = "default"`. Note: as of UNITE v10.0, separate
83	#'   static/dynamic archives are not available for `taxon_group = "fungi"`;
84	#'   both options download the same archive.
85	#' @param taxon_group (Character, default `"fungi"`) One of `"fungi"` or
86	#'   `"eukaryotes"`.
87	#' @param version (Character, default `"10.0"`) UNITE version. Use `"10.0"`
88	#'   for the 2024 release.
89	#' @param taxonomic_format (Character, default `"default"`) One of
90	#'   `"default"` or `"sintax"`. When `"default"`, downloads the general
91	#'   FASTA release (`.tgz` archive with `k__`/`p__` taxonomy). When
92	#'   `"sintax"`, downloads a single FASTA file already formatted for
93	#'   VSEARCH SINTAX classification (with `tax=d:...` headers).
94	#' @param doi (Character) If provided, overrides version-based URL
95	#'   construction with a direct DOI download. Useful for older or
96	#'   alternative releases.
97	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
98	#'
99	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
100	#' @export
101	#' @author Adrien Taudière
102	#' @details
103	#' When `taxonomic_format = "default"`: since UNITE v10.0, the `.tgz`
104	#' archive contains all clustering variants (dynamic, 97%, 99%) bundled
105	#' together. After downloading, extract the archive and select the
106	#' appropriate FASTA file.
107	#'
108	#' When `taxonomic_format = "sintax"`: the downloaded `.gz` file is a
109	#' single FASTA with SINTAX-ready taxonomy headers. This file can be used
110	#' directly with `vsearch --sintax`.
111	#'
112	#' The S3 download URLs use opaque UUIDs that change between releases.
113	#' If the automatic URL fails, visit
114	#' <https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php> to find the current DOI and pass
115	#' it via the `doi` parameter, or download manually.
116	#'
117	#' Please cite UNITE: Abarenkov K et al. (2024) UNITE general FASTA
118	#' release for Fungi. UNITE Community.
119	#' \doi{10.15156/BIO/2959336}
120	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()]
121	#' @examples
122	#' \dontrun{
123	#' # Download UNITE v10.0 default (general FASTA archive) for fungi
124	#' download_unite_db(dest_dir = "databases")
125	#'
126	#' # Download UNITE v10.0 SINTAX-formatted for fungi
127	#' download_unite_db(dest_dir = "databases", taxonomic_format = "sintax")
128	#'
129	#' # Download for all eukaryotes in SINTAX format
130	#' download_unite_db(
131	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
132	#'   taxon_group = "eukaryotes",
133	#'   taxonomic_format = "sintax"
134	#' )
135	#' }
136	download_unite_db <- function(
137	  dest_dir = ".",
138	  type = c("dynamic", "static"),
139	  taxon_group = c("fungi", "eukaryotes"),
140	  version = "10.0",
141	  taxonomic_format = c("default", "sintax"),
142	  doi = NULL,
143	  verbose = TRUE
144	) {
145	  type <- match.arg(type)
146	  taxon_group <- match.arg(taxon_group)
147	  taxonomic_format <- match.arg(taxonomic_format)
148	
149	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
150	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
151	  }
152	
153	  if (!is.null(doi)) {
154	    # User-provided DOI: resolve to download URL
155	    url <- paste0("https://doi.org/", sub("^https?://doi.org/", "", doi))
156	    ext <- ifelse(taxonomic_format == "sintax", ".gz", ".tgz")
157	    dest_file <- file.path(
158	      dest_dir,
159	      paste0("UNITE_", type, "_", taxon_group, ext)
160	    )
161	    message(
162	      "Using user-provided DOI. You may need to follow redirects manually.\n",
163	      "Consider downloading from https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php"
164	    )
165	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
166	    return(invisible(dest_file))
167	  }
168	
169	  if (taxonomic_format == "sintax") {
170	    # Known S3 download URLs for UNITE v10.0 SINTAX FASTA release
171	    # Source: nf-core/ampliseq ref_databases.config (sintax_ref_taxonomy)
172	    # Note: S3 UUIDs are opaque and change between releases
173	    sintax_urls <- list(
174	      fungi = paste0(
175	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
176	        "b27cffec-1e7d-4584-93d3-12add9fa180b.gz"
177	      ),
178	      eukaryotes = paste0(
179	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
180	        "6f19ddb6-1ac0-4834-a74c-b639688878a4.gz"
181	      )
182	    )
183	
184	    url <- sintax_urls[[taxon_group]]
185	
186	    if (is.null(url)) {
187	      stop(
188	        "No known SINTAX download URL for UNITE version ",
189	        version,
190	        " (",
191	        taxon_group,
192	        ").\n",
193	        "Visit https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php for manual download.",
194	        call. = FALSE
195	      )
196	    }
197	
198	    dest_file <- file.path(
199	      dest_dir,
200	      paste0(
201	        "sh_general_release_sintax_",
202	        ifelse(taxon_group == "fungi", "s_", "s_all_"),
203	        gsub("\\.", "", version),
204	        ".fasta.gz"
205	      )
206	    )
207	
208	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
209	
210	    if (verbose) {
211	      message(
212	        "UNITE SINTAX database saved as: ",
213	        dest_file,
214	        "\n",
215	        "This file can be used directly with: ",
216	        "vsearch --sintax reads.fasta --db ",
217	        dest_file,
218	        " --tabbedout out.txt --sintax_cutoff 0.8"
219	      )
220	    }
221	
222	    return(invisible(dest_file))
223	  }
224	
225	  # Known S3 download URLs for UNITE v10.0 general FASTA release
226	
227	  # Source: nf-core/ampliseq ref_databases.config
228	  # The .tgz archives contain dynamic, 97%, and 99% clustering variants
229	  # Note: S3 UUIDs are opaque and change between releases
230	  unite_urls <- list(
231	    fungi = list(
232	      dynamic = paste0(
233	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
234	        "d18aa648-3f4c-4f46-84d4-c8c5d48439ba.tgz"
235	      ),
236	      static = paste0(
237	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
238	        "d18aa648-3f4c-4f46-84d4-c8c5d48439ba.tgz"
239	      )
240	    ),
241	    eukaryotes = list(
242	      dynamic = paste0(
243	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
244	        "1dda2021-4893-4f2f-b50e-87bfea795267.tgz"
245	      ),
246	      static = paste0(
247	        "https://s3.hpc.ut.ee/plutof-public/original/",
248	        "1dda2021-4893-4f2f-b50e-87bfea795267.tgz"
249	      )
250	    )
251	  )
252	
253	  if (taxon_group == "fungi" && type == "static") {
254	    message(
255	      "Note: UNITE v10.0 does not ship separate static/dynamic archives for ",
256	      "fungi. Downloading the single available fungi archive."
257	    )
258	  }
259	
260	  url <- unite_urls[[taxon_group]][[type]]
261	
262	  if (is.null(url)) {
263	    stop(
264	      "No known download URL for UNITE version ",
265	      version,
266	      " (",
267	      type,
268	      ", ",
269	      taxon_group,
270	      ").\n",
271	      "Visit https://unite.ut.ee/repository.php for manual download.",
272	      call. = FALSE
273	    )
274	  }
275	
276	  dest_file <- file.path(
277	    dest_dir,
278	    paste0(
279	      "sh_general_release_",
280	      type,
281	      "_",
282	      ifelse(taxon_group == "fungi", "s_", "s_all_"),
283	      gsub("\\.", "", version),
284	      ".tgz"
285	    )
286	  )
287	
288	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
289	
290	  if (verbose) {
291	    message(
292	      "UNITE database saved as: ",
293	      dest_file,
294	      "\n",
295	      "Extract with: untar('",
296	      dest_file,
297	      "', exdir = '",
298	      dest_dir,
299	      "')"
300	    )
301	  }
302	
303	  invisible(dest_file)
304	}

307	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
308	# SILVA
309	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
310	
311	#' Download a SILVA reference database
312	#'
313	#' @description
314	#' Downloads the SILVA ribosomal RNA database (16S/18S). By default,
315	#' downloads the dada2-formatted training sets from Zenodo (maintained by
316	#' Benjamin Callahan). Alternatively, downloads raw SILVA exports from
317	#' arb-silva.de.
318	#'
319	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
320	#'   downloaded file.
321	#' @param version (Character, default `"138.2"`) SILVA version number.
322	#' @param target (Character, default `"SSU"`) One of `"SSU"` or `"LSU"`.
323	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
324	#'   - `"dada2"`: dada2-formatted training set from Zenodo (NR99,
325	#'     recommended for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`).
326	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species assignment file for
327	#'     `dada2::addSpecies()`.
328	#'   - `"raw"`: raw SILVA NR99 FASTA with taxonomy from arb-silva.de.
329	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
330	#'
331	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
332	#' @export
333	#' @author Adrien Taudière
334	#' @details
335	#' The dada2-formatted files are maintained by Benjamin Callahan on Zenodo
336	#' and are the recommended format for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()` and
337	#' `dada2::addSpecies()`. See
338	#' <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for details.
339	#'
340	#' SILVA data is free for academic use. Commercial use requires a license.
341	#' See <https://www.arb-silva.de/silva-license-information/>.
342	#'
343	#' Please cite: Quast C et al. (2013) The SILVA ribosomal RNA gene
344	#' database project. Nucleic Acids Research 41:D590-D596.
345	#' \doi{10.1093/nar/gks1219}
346	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()]
347	#' @examples
348	#' \dontrun{
349	#' # Download dada2-formatted SILVA for assignTaxonomy()
350	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases")
351	#'
352	#' # Download species assignment file
353	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "dada2_species")
354	#'
355	#' # Download raw SILVA NR99 FASTA
356	#' download_silva_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "raw")
357	#' }
358	download_silva_db <- function(
359	  dest_dir = ".",
360	  version = "138.2",
361	  target = c("SSU", "LSU"),
362	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "raw"),
363	  verbose = TRUE
364	) {
365	  target <- match.arg(target)
366	  format <- match.arg(format)
367	
368	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
369	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
370	  }
371	
372	  # Zenodo record IDs for dada2-formatted SILVA
373	  zenodo_records <- list(
374	    "138.2" = "14169026",
375	    "138.1" = "4587955"
376	  )
377	
378	  # Version string for URLs (138.2 -> 138_2 for arb-silva paths)
379	  version_us <- gsub("\\.", "_", version)
380	
381	  if (format %in% c("dada2", "dada2_species")) {
382	    if (target == "LSU") {
383	      stop(
384	        "dada2-formatted files are only available for SSU. ",
385	        "Use format = 'raw' for LSU downloads.",
386	        call. = FALSE
387	      )
388	    }
389	
390	    record_id <- zenodo_records[[version]]
391	    if (is.null(record_id)) {
392	      stop(
393	        "No known Zenodo record for SILVA v",
394	        version,
395	        ". ",
396	        "Known versions: ",
397	        paste(names(zenodo_records), collapse = ", "),
398	        ". Visit https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html",
399	        call. = FALSE
400	      )
401	    }
402	
403	    if (format == "dada2") {
404	      filename <- paste0("silva_nr99_v", version, "_toSpecies_trainset.fa.gz")
405	    } else {
406	      filename <- paste0("silva_v", version, "_assignSpecies.fa.gz")
407	    }
408	
409	    url <- paste0(
410	      "https://zenodo.org/records/",
411	      record_id,
412	      "/files/",
413	      filename
414	    )
415	  } else {
416	    # Raw SILVA export from arb-silva.de
417	    target_full <- paste0(target, "Ref_NR99")
418	    filename <- paste0(
419	      "SILVA_",
420	      version,
421	      "_",
422	      target_full,
423	      "_tax_silva_trunc.fasta.gz"
424	    )
425	    url <- paste0(
426	      "https://www.arb-silva.de/fileadmin/silva_databases/",
427	      "release_",
428	      version_us,
429	      "/Exports/",
430	      filename
431	    )
432	  }
433	
434	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
435	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
436	
437	  invisible(dest_file)
438	}

441	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
442	# PR2
443	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
444	
445	#' Download a PR2 reference database
446	#'
447	#' @description
448	#' Downloads the PR2 protist ribosomal reference database from GitHub
449	#' releases. PR2 provides 18S rRNA gene sequences for protists and other
450	#' eukaryotes.
451	#'
452	#' For more advanced access to PR2 data (e.g., full taxonomy tables,
453	#' metadata, or custom queries), see the
454	#' [pr2database](https://pr2database.github.io/pr2database/) R package.
455	#'
456	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
457	#'   downloaded file.
458	#' @param version (Character) PR2 version number (e.g., `"5.0.0"`). If
459	#'   `NULL` (default), the latest release is fetched from GitHub.
460	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of `"dada2"`, `"mothur"`,
461	#'   `"UTAX"`, or `"sintax"` (alias for `"UTAX"`).
462	#' @param marker (Character, default `"SSU"`) One of `"SSU"` or `"plastid"`.
463	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
464	#'
465	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
466	#' @export
467	#' @author Adrien Taudière
468	#' @details
469	#' PR2 releases are hosted on GitHub at
470	#' <https://github.com/pr2database/pr2database/releases>.
471	#'
472	#' Please cite: Guillou L et al. (2013) The Protist Ribosomal Reference
473	#' database (PR2). Nucleic Acids Research 41:D1108-D1113.
474	#' \doi{10.1093/nar/gks1160}
475	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [format2sintax()],
476	#'   [pr2database](https://pr2database.github.io/pr2database/) R package
477	#' @examples
478	#' \dontrun{
479	#' # Download latest PR2 in dada2 format
480	#' download_pr2_db(dest_dir = "databases")
481	#'
482	#' # Download specific version in UTAX format
483	#' download_pr2_db(dest_dir = "databases", version = "5.0.0", format = "UTAX")
484	#' }
485	download_pr2_db <- function(
486	  dest_dir = ".",
487	  version = NULL,
488	  format = c("dada2", "mothur", "UTAX", "sintax"),
489	  marker = c("SSU", "plastid"),
490	  verbose = TRUE
491	) {
492	  format <- match.arg(format)
493	  # "sintax" is an alias for "UTAX" (same format, different naming conventions)
494	  if (format == "sintax") {
495	    format <- "UTAX"
496	  }
497	  marker <- match.arg(marker)
498	
499	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
500	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
501	  }
502	
503	  # Get latest version from GitHub API if not specified
504	  if (is.null(version)) {
505	    if (verbose) {
506	      message("Querying GitHub for latest PR2 release...")
507	    }
508	    api_url <- paste0(
509	      "https://api.github.com/repos/pr2database/pr2database/",
510	      "releases/latest"
511	    )
512	    response <- tryCatch(
513	      jsonlite::fromJSON(api_url),
514	      error = function(e) {
515	        stop(
516	          "Could not query GitHub API for latest PR2 version. ",
517	          "Specify version manually (e.g., version = '5.0.0').\n",
518	          "Error: ",
519	          conditionMessage(e),
520	          call. = FALSE
521	        )
522	      }
523	    )
524	    version <- sub("^v", "", response$tag_name)
525	    if (verbose) {
526	      message("Latest PR2 version: ", version)
527	    }
528	  }
529	
530	  # Construct filename
531	  # Pattern: pr2_version_{VERSION}_SSU_dada2.fasta.gz
532	  marker_str <- ifelse(marker == "SSU", "SSU", "plastid_16S")
533	  filename <- paste0(
534	    "pr2_version_",
535	    version,
536	    "_",
537	    marker_str,
538	    "_",
539	    format,
540	    ".fasta.gz"
541	  )
542	
543	  url <- paste0(
544	    "https://github.com/pr2database/pr2database/releases/download/",
545	    "v",
546	    version,
547	    "/",
548	    filename
549	  )
550	
551	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
552	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
553	
554	  invisible(dest_file)
555	}
556

558	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
559	# BOLD
560	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
561	
562	#' Download sequences from BOLD Systems
563	#'
564	#' @description
565	#' Downloads reference sequences from BOLD Systems (Barcode of Life Data)
566	#' for a given taxonomic group. Unlike other databases, BOLD does not
567	#' provide a single pre-built reference FASTA — sequences are queried by
568	#' taxon via the BOLD API.
569	#'
570	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
571	#'   downloaded file.
572	#' @param taxon (Character, required) Taxonomic name to query (e.g.,
573	#'   `"Fungi"`, `"Arthropoda"`, `"Mammalia"`).
574	#' @param marker (Character, default `"COI-5P"`) The barcode marker. Common
575	#'   values: `"COI-5P"`, `"ITS"`, `"matK"`, `"rbcL"`.
576	#' @param output_format (Character, default `"fasta"`) Output format.
577	#'   Currently only `"fasta"` is supported.
578	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
579	#'
580	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
581	#' @export
582	#' @author Adrien Taudière
583	#' @details
584	#' This function uses the BOLD v3 public API. For very large taxonomic
585	#' groups, the download may take a long time or fail due to server limits.
586	#' In such cases, consider using narrower taxonomic queries or the
587	#' [bold](https://docs.ropensci.org/bold/) R package.
588	#'
589	#' Please cite: Ratnasingham S & Hebert PDN (2007) BOLD: The Barcode of
590	#' Life Data System. Molecular Ecology Notes 7:355-364.
591	#' \doi{10.1111/j.1471-8286.2007.01678.x}
592	#' @examples
593	#' \dontrun{
594	#' # Download COI sequences for Fungi
595	#' download_bold_db(dest_dir = "databases", taxon = "Fungi")
596	#'
597	#' # Download ITS sequences for a specific order
598	#' download_bold_db(
599	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
600	#'   taxon = "Agaricales",
601	#'   marker = "ITS"
602	#' )
603	#' }
604	download_bold_db <- function(
605	  dest_dir = ".",
606	  taxon = NULL,
607	  marker = "COI-5P",
608	  output_format = "fasta",
609	  verbose = TRUE
610	) {
611	  if (is.null(taxon)) {
612	    stop("You must specify a taxon (e.g., taxon = 'Fungi').", call. = FALSE)
613	  }
614	
615	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
616	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
617	  }
618	
619	  # BOLD v3 API for sequence downloads
620	  url <- paste0(
621	    "http://v3.boldsystems.org/index.php/API_Public/sequence",
622	    "?taxon=",
623	    utils::URLencode(taxon, reserved = TRUE)
624	  )
625	
626	  filename <- paste0(
627	    "BOLD_",
628	    gsub(" ", "_", taxon),
629	    "_",
630	    marker,
631	    ".fasta"
632	  )
633	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
634	
635	  if (verbose) {
636	    message(
637	      "Querying BOLD API for '",
638	      taxon,
639	      "' sequences...\n",
640	      "Note: large taxonomic groups may take several minutes."
641	    )
642	  }
643	
644	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
645	
646	  # The BOLD API returns all markers; filter by marker if needed
647	  if (verbose) {
648	    n_seq <- count_pattern_db(dest_file, pattern = ">")
649	    message("Downloaded ", n_seq, " sequences for '", taxon, "'")
650	    message(
651	      "Note: BOLD API returns all markers. You may want to filter ",
652	      "for '",
653	      marker,
654	      "' using filter_db()."
655	    )
656	  }
657	
658	  invisible(dest_file)
659	}
660

662	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
663	# MaarjAM
664	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
665	
666	#' Download the MaarjAM reference database
667	#'
668	#' @description
669	#' Downloads the MaarjAM database of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (AMF)
670	#' virtual taxa (VT) sequences (18S rDNA). The database is maintained at
671	#' the University of Tartu.
672	#'
673	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
674	#'   downloaded file.
675	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL for the MaarjAM FASTA file.
676	#'   Defaults to the known download endpoint. Override if the URL has
677	#'   changed.
678	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
679	#'
680	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
681	#' @export
682	#' @author Adrien Taudière
683	#' @details
684	#' MaarjAM does not have a versioned API. The download URL may change.
685	#' If the default URL fails, visit <https://maarjam.ut.ee/?action=bDownload>
686	#' to find the current download link and pass it via the `url` parameter.
687	#'
688	#' Please cite: Opik M et al. (2010) The online database MaarjAM reveals
689	#' global and ecosystemic distribution patterns in arbuscular mycorrhizal
690	#' fungi (Glomeromycota). New Phytologist 188:223-241.
691	#' \doi{10.1111/j.1469-8137.2010.03334.x}
692	#' @examples
693	#' \dontrun{
694	#' download_marjaam_db(dest_dir = "databases")
695	#' }
696	download_marjaam_db <- function(
697	  dest_dir = ".",
698	  url = "https://maarjam.ut.ee/resources/maarjam_database.fasta",
699	  verbose = TRUE
700	) {
701	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
702	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
703	  }
704	
705	  filename <- "maarjam_database.fasta"
706	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
707	
708	  tryCatch(
709	    download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose),
710	    error = function(e) {
711	      stop(
712	        "MaarjAM download failed. The URL may have changed.\n",
713	        "Visit https://maarjam.ut.ee/?action=bDownload to find the ",
714	        "current download link, then use:\n",
715	        "  download_marjaam_db(url = 'https://...')\n",
716	        "Original error: ",
717	        conditionMessage(e),
718	        call. = FALSE
719	      )
720	    }
721	  )
722	
723	  invisible(dest_file)
724	}
725

727	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
728	# Eukaryome
729	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
730	
731	#' Download the Eukaryome reference database
732	#'
733	#' @description
734	#' Downloads the Eukaryome database for eukaryotic organisms. Supports
735	#' multiple markers (SSU 18S, ITS, LSU 28S) and output formats (general
736	#' FASTA, dada2, mothur).
737	#'
738	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
739	#'   downloaded file.
740	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL for the Eukaryome file.
741	#'   Override if the URL has changed. If `NULL` (default), the function
742	#'   directs you to the Eukaryome download page.
743	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
744	#'
745	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly), or a message
746	#'   with download instructions if no URL is provided.
747	#' @export
748	#' @author Adrien Taudière
749	#' @details
750	#' Eukaryome does not provide a stable programmatic download API. Files
751	#' are available at <https://eukaryome.org/download/> in several formats:
752	#'
753	#' - General FASTA: <https://eukaryome.org/generalfasta/>
754	#' - dada2 format: <https://eukaryome.org/dada2/>
755	#' - mothur format: <https://eukaryome.org/mothur/>
756	#' - SINTAX format: <https://eukaryome.org/sintax/>
757	#' - QIIME2 format: <https://eukaryome.org/qiime2/>
758	#'
759	#' Visit one of these pages, copy the direct download link, and pass it
760	#' via the `url` parameter.
761	#'
762	#' Please cite: Vasar M et al. (2024) Eukaryome: the rRNA gene reference
763	#' database for identification of all eukaryotes. Database.
764	#' \doi{10.1093/database/baae043}
765	#' @examples
766	#' \dontrun{
767	#' # Download with a specific URL from the Eukaryome website
768	#' download_eukaryome_db(
769	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
770	#'   url = "https://eukaryome.org/files/eukaryome_v1.9_SSU_dada2.fasta.gz"
771	#' )
772	#' }
773	download_eukaryome_db <- function(
774	  dest_dir = ".",
775	  url = NULL,
776	  verbose = TRUE
777	) {
778	  if (is.null(url)) {
779	    message(
780	      "Eukaryome does not provide stable programmatic download URLs.\n",
781	      "Please visit one of these pages to get the download link:\n",
782	      "  - General FASTA: https://eukaryome.org/generalfasta/\n",
783	      "  - dada2 format:  https://eukaryome.org/dada2/\n",
784	      "  - mothur format: https://eukaryome.org/mothur/\n",
785	      "  - SINTAX format: https://eukaryome.org/sintax/\n",
786	      "  - QIIME2 format: https://eukaryome.org/qiime2/\n\n",
787	      "Then call:\n",
788	      "  download_eukaryome_db(url = 'https://...')"
789	    )
790	    return(invisible(NULL))
791	  }
792	
793	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
794	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
795	  }
796	
797	  filename <- basename(url)
798	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
799	
800	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
801	
802	  invisible(dest_file)
803	}
804

806	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
807	# Greengenes2
808	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
809	
810	#' Download a Greengenes2 reference database
811	#'
812	#' @description
813	#' Downloads the Greengenes2 16S rRNA database. By default, downloads the
814	#' dada2-formatted training sets from Zenodo (maintained by Benjamin
815	#' Callahan). Alternatively, downloads backbone sequences from the
816	#' Greengenes2 FTP server.
817	#'
818	#' Note that Greengenes2 uses `d__` (domain) instead of `k__` (kingdom)
819	#' as the first rank prefix. Use `tax_format = "greengenes2"` with
820	#' [summarize_db()] and [list_ranks_db()] for correct parsing.
821	#'
822	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
823	#'   downloaded file.
824	#' @param version (Character, default `"2024.09"`) Greengenes2 version
825	#'   in `YYYY.MM` format.
826	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
827	#'   - `"dada2"`: dada2-formatted training set from Zenodo (recommended
828	#'     for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`).
829	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species-level training set for
830	#'     `dada2::assignTaxonomy()` (includes species).
831	#'   - `"fasta"`: plain FASTA sequences from the FTP server.
832	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
833	#'
834	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
835	#' @export
836	#' @author Adrien Taudière
837	#' @details
838	#' The dada2-formatted files are maintained by Benjamin Callahan on Zenodo
839	#' and are the same source as the SILVA dada2 training sets. See
840	#' <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for details.
841	#'
842	#' Please cite: McDonald D et al. (2024) Greengenes2 unifies microbial
843	#' data in a single reference tree. Nature Biotechnology 42:715-718.
844	#' \doi{10.1038/s41587-023-01845-1}
845	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [tax_prefixes()]
846	#' @examples
847	#' \dontrun{
848	#' # Download dada2-formatted Greengenes2
849	#' download_greengenes2_db(dest_dir = "databases")
850	#'
851	#' # Download plain FASTA from FTP
852	#' download_greengenes2_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "fasta")
853	#' }
854	download_greengenes2_db <- function(
855	  dest_dir = ".",
856	  version = "2024.09",
857	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "fasta"),
858	  verbose = TRUE
859	) {
860	  format <- match.arg(format)
861	
862	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
863	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
864	  }
865	
866	  # Zenodo record IDs for dada2-formatted Greengenes2
867	  zenodo_records <- list(
868	    "2024.09" = "14169078"
869	  )
870	
871	  version_us <- gsub("\\.", "_", version)
872	
873	  if (format %in% c("dada2", "dada2_species")) {
874	    record_id <- zenodo_records[[version]]
875	    if (is.null(record_id)) {
876	      stop(
877	        "No known Zenodo record for Greengenes2 v",
878	        version,
879	        ". ",
880	        "Known versions: ",
881	        paste(names(zenodo_records), collapse = ", "),
882	        ". Visit https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html",
883	        call. = FALSE
884	      )
885	    }
886	
887	    if (format == "dada2") {
888	      filename <- paste0("gg2_", version_us, "_toGenus_trainset.fa.gz")
889	    } else {
890	      filename <- paste0("gg2_", version_us, "_toSpecies_trainset.fa.gz")
891	    }
892	
893	    url <- paste0(
894	      "https://zenodo.org/records/",
895	      record_id,
896	      "/files/",
897	      filename
898	    )
899	  } else {
900	    # Plain FASTA from Greengenes2 FTP
901	    filename <- paste0(version, ".seqs.fna.gz")
902	    url <- paste0(
903	      "https://ftp.microbio.me/greengenes_release/",
904	      version,
905	      "/",
906	      filename
907	    )
908	  }
909	
910	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
911	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
912	
913	  invisible(dest_file)
914	}
915	
916	
917	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
918	# RDP
919	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
920	
921	#' Download an RDP reference database
922	#'
923	#' @description
924	#' Downloads the Ribosomal Database Project (RDP) 16S rRNA database.
925	#' By default, downloads the dada2-formatted training sets from Zenodo
926	#' (maintained by Benjamin Callahan).
927	#'
928	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
929	#'   downloaded file.
930	#' @param trainset (Character, default `"19"`) RDP trainset version number.
931	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of:
932	#'   - `"dada2"`: training set for `dada2::assignTaxonomy()`.
933	#'   - `"dada2_species"`: species assignment file for
934	#'     `dada2::addSpecies()`.
935	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
936	#'
937	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly).
938	#' @export
939	#' @author Adrien Taudière
940	#' @details
941	#' The dada2-formatted files are maintained by Benjamin Callahan on
942	#' Zenodo. See <https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html> for
943	#' details.
944	#'
945	#' Please cite: Cole JR et al. (2014) Ribosomal Database Project: data
946	#' and tools for high throughput rRNA analysis. Nucleic Acids Research
947	#' 42:D633-D642. \doi{10.1093/nar/gkt1244}
948	#' @seealso [format2dada2()], [download_silva_db()]
949	#' @examples
950	#' \dontrun{
951	#' # Download RDP trainset 19 for assignTaxonomy()
952	#' download_rdp_db(dest_dir = "databases")
953	#'
954	#' # Download species assignment file
955	#' download_rdp_db(dest_dir = "databases", format = "dada2_species")
956	#' }
957	download_rdp_db <- function(
958	  dest_dir = ".",
959	  trainset = "19",
960	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species"),
961	  verbose = TRUE
962	) {
963	  format <- match.arg(format)
964	
965	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
966	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
967	  }
968	
969	  # Zenodo record IDs for dada2-formatted RDP
970	  zenodo_records <- list(
971	    "19" = "14168771",
972	    "18" = "4310151",
973	    "16" = "801828"
974	  )
975	
976	  record_id <- zenodo_records[[trainset]]
977	  if (is.null(record_id)) {
978	    stop(
979	      "No known Zenodo record for RDP trainset ",
980	      trainset,
981	      ". ",
982	      "Known trainsets: ",
983	      paste(names(zenodo_records), collapse = ", "),
984	      ". Visit https://benjjneb.github.io/dada2/training.html",
985	      call. = FALSE
986	    )
987	  }
988	
989	  if (format == "dada2") {
990	    filename <- paste0("rdp_train_set_", trainset, ".fa.gz")
991	  } else {
992	    filename <- paste0("rdp_species_assignment_", trainset, ".fa.gz")
993	  }
994	
995	  url <- paste0(
996	    "https://zenodo.org/records/",
997	    record_id,
998	    "/files/",
999	    filename
1000	  )
1001	
1002	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
1003	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
1004	
1005	  invisible(dest_file)
1006	}
1007	
1008	
1009	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1010	# MIDORI2
1011	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1012	
1013	#' Download a MIDORI2 reference database
1014	#'
1015	#' @description
1016	#' Downloads the MIDORI2 reference database for eukaryotic mitochondrial
1017	#' genes (COI, 12S, 16S, Cytb, etc.). MIDORI2 provides pre-formatted
1018	#' FASTA files for multiple classifiers (dada2, SINTAX, RDP, BLAST).
1019	#'
1020	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
1021	#'   downloaded file.
1022	#' @param gene (Character, default `"CO1"`) Mitochondrial gene marker.
1023	#'   Common values: `"CO1"`, `"srRNA"` (12S), `"lrRNA"` (16S), `"Cytb"`.
1024	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of `"dada2"`,
1025	#'   `"dada2_species"`, `"SINTAX"`, `"RDP"`, or `"BLAST"`.
1026	#' @param seq_type (Character, default `"UNIQ"`) One of `"UNIQ"` (all
1027	#'   unique haplotypes per species) or `"LONGEST"` (single longest
1028	#'   sequence per species).
1029	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL. If `NULL` (default), the
1030	#'   function provides instructions and the download page URL.
1031	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
1032	#'
1033	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly), or a message
1034	#'   with download instructions if no URL is provided.
1035	#' @export
1036	#' @author Adrien Taudière
1037	#' @details
1038	#' MIDORI2 download URLs include a date-stamped directory path that
1039	#' changes with each GenBank release, making fully programmatic access
1040	#' fragile. Visit <https://www.reference-midori.info/download.php> to
1041	#' find the current download URL for your desired gene and format, then
1042	#' pass it via the `url` parameter.
1043	#'
1044	#' Files are typically named following this pattern:
1045	#' `MIDORI2_{TYPE}_NUC_SP_GB{VERSION}_{GENE}_{FORMAT}.fasta.gz`
1046	#'
1047	#' Please cite: Leray M et al. (2022) MIDORI2: A collection of quality
1048	#' controlled, preformatted, and regularly updated reference databases
1049	#' for taxonomic assignment of eukaryotic mitochondrial sequences.
1050	#' Environmental DNA 4:894-907. \doi{10.1002/edn3.303}
1051	#' @seealso [format2sintax()], [format2dada2()]
1052	#' @examples
1053	#' \dontrun{
1054	#' # Get instructions for downloading MIDORI2
1055	#' download_midori2_db()
1056	#'
1057	#' # Download with a specific URL
1058	#' download_midori2_db(
1059	#'   dest_dir = "databases",
1060	#'   url = "https://reference-midori.info/download/Databases/..."
1061	#' )
1062	#' }
1063	download_midori2_db <- function(
1064	  dest_dir = ".",
1065	  gene = "CO1",
1066	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "SINTAX", "RDP", "BLAST"),
1067	  seq_type = c("UNIQ", "LONGEST"),
1068	  url = NULL,
1069	  verbose = TRUE
1070	) {
1071	  format <- match.arg(format)
1072	  seq_type <- match.arg(seq_type)
1073	
1074	  if (is.null(url)) {
1075	    format_dir <- switch(
1076	      format,
1077	      dada2 = "DADA2",
1078	      dada2_species = "DADA2_sp",
1079	      SINTAX = "SINTAX",
1080	      RDP = "RDP",
1081	      BLAST = "BLAST"
1082	    )
1083	    message(
1084	      "MIDORI2 download URLs change with each GenBank release.\n",
1085	      "Please visit the download page to get the URL for your desired ",
1086	      "gene and format:\n",
1087	      "  https://www.reference-midori.info/download.php\n\n",
1088	      "Look for a file matching this pattern:\n",
1089	      "  MIDORI2_",
1090	      seq_type,
1091	      "_NUC_SP_GB{VERSION}_",
1092	      gene,
1093	      "_",
1094	      toupper(format_dir),
1095	      ".fasta.gz\n\n",
1096	      "Then call:\n",
1097	      "  download_midori2_db(url = 'https://...')"
1098	    )
1099	    return(invisible(NULL))
1100	  }
1101	
1102	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
1103	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
1104	  }
1105	
1106	  filename <- basename(url)
1107	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
1108	
1109	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
1110	
1111	  invisible(dest_file)
1112	}
1113	
1114	
1115	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1116	# DIAT.barcode
1117	# ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
1118	
1119	#' Download a Diat.barcode reference database
1120	#'
1121	#' @description
1122	#' Downloads the Diat.barcode reference database for diatom rbcL barcoding.
1123	#' This is a curated database for diatom identification using the rbcL
1124	#' marker gene. The recommended access method is through the
1125	#' [diatbarcode](https://github.com/fkeck/diatbarcode) R package, which
1126	#' provides additional tools for working with the database.
1127	#'
1128	#' @param dest_dir (Character, default `"."`) Directory to save the
1129	#'   downloaded file.
1130	#' @param format (Character, default `"dada2"`) One of `"dada2"` or
1131	#'   `"dada2_species"`. Uses dada2-formatted files from the INRAE Dataverse.
1132	#' @param url (Character) Direct download URL. If `NULL` (default), the
1133	#'   function provides instructions for using the `diatbarcode` R package.
1134	#' @param verbose (Logical, default `TRUE`) Print progress messages.
1135	#'
1136	#' @returns The path to the downloaded file (invisibly), or a message
1137	#'   with download instructions if no URL is provided.
1138	#' @export
1139	#' @author Adrien Taudière
1140	#' @details
1141	#' The Diat.barcode database is maintained by INRAE and hosted on
1142	#' Recherche Data Gouv. For more advanced access (metadata, full taxonomy
1143	#' tables, custom queries), consider using the
1144	#' [diatbarcode](https://github.com/fkeck/diatbarcode) R package
1145	#' directly:
1146	#'
1147	#' ```
1148	#' diatbarcode::download_diatbarcode(
1149	#'   path = "databases",
1150	#'   flavor = "rbcl312_dada2_tax"
1151	#' )
1152	#' ```
1153	#'
1154	#' Please cite: Rimet F et al. (2019) Diat.barcode, an open-access
1155	#' curated barcode library for diatoms. Scientific Reports 9:15116.
1156	#' \doi{10.1038/s41598-019-51500-6}
1157	#' @examples
1158	#' \dontrun{
1159	#' download_diatbarcode_db(dest_dir = "databases")
1160	#' }
1161	download_diatbarcode_db <- function(
1162	  dest_dir = ".",
1163	  format = c("dada2", "dada2_species"),
1164	  url = NULL,
1165	  verbose = TRUE
1166	) {
1167	  format <- match.arg(format)
1168	
1169	  if (!dir.exists(dest_dir)) {
1170	    dir.create(dest_dir, recursive = TRUE)
1171	  }
1172	
1173	  if (is.null(url)) {
1174	    message(
1175	      "Diat.barcode files are hosted on INRAE Dataverse.\n",
1176	      "The recommended approach is to use the diatbarcode R package:\n",
1177	      "  diatbarcode::download_diatbarcode(\n",
1178	      "    path = '",
1179	      dest_dir,
1180	      "',\n",
1181	      "    flavor = '",
1182	      ifelse(format == "dada2", "rbcl312_dada2_tax", "rbcl312_dada2_spe"),
1183	      "'\n",
1184	      "  )\n\n",
1185	      "Alternatively, visit:\n",
1186	      "  https://entrepot.recherche.data.gouv.fr/dataset.xhtml",
1187	      "?persistentId=doi:10.15454/HNI1EK\n",
1188	      "and pass the direct file download URL via the `url` parameter."
1189	    )
1190	    return(invisible(NULL))
1191	  }
1192	
1193	  filename <- basename(url)
1194	  dest_file <- file.path(dest_dir, filename)
1195	
1196	  download_file(url, dest_file, verbose = verbose)
1197	
1198	  invisible(dest_file)
1199	}
